claim: The zodiac section's anomalously low entropy (3.7149 bits, lowest of all sections) is caused by a label-repetition encoding scheme in which a small set of high-frequency label words are systematically repeated around circular diagrams, and the section's word-frequency distribution is therefore best fit by a truncated Zipf distribution rather than a full Zipf law.
evidence_for: Zodiac section has only 1,297 words across 12 folios â€” the smallest vocabulary-to-folio ratio, consistent with repetitive labeling
Entropy 3.7149 bits is 0.19 bits below the next-lowest section (astronomical 3.7471), a large gap suggesting a qualitatively different text mode
Label-based texts (e.g., calendar month names, zodiac sign labels) in medieval manuscripts characteristically repeat short high-frequency tokens around diagrams
Top-10 words globally are dominated by short tokens (ol=553, ar=402, or=378, y=329); these could be label words concentrated in zodiac section
evidence_against: Without section-level word-frequency breakdown it is not confirmed that top words are disproportionately concentrated in zodiac
Low entropy could also reflect a simpler or weaker cipher applied to normal prose
Cosmological section (10 folios, 2,199 words, 3.8515 bits) has similar folio count but much higher entropy, suggesting folio count alone doesn't drive entropy
test: Extract the word-frequency distribution for the zodiac section alone. Fit both a standard Zipf distribution and a truncated Zipf (with vocabulary cutoff V) to the zodiac word-frequency data using maximum likelihood. Compare AIC/BIC of both fits. Separately, compute the type-token ratio and hapax ratio for zodiac vs. all other sections.
test_metric: AIC difference between truncated vs. standard Zipf fit on zodiac word frequencies; zodiac hapax ratio vs. mean hapax ratio of other sections; zodiac type-token ratio
test_threshold: AIC improvement > 4 for truncated Zipf would favor label-encoding hypothesis; zodiac hapax ratio more than 1.5 standard deviations below the mean of other sections would independently support label repetition

tests_run.test: extract the word-frequency distribution for the zodiac section alone. fit both a standard zipf distr
tests_run.details: Observed Zipf exponent: 0.8946 (R²=0.9084). Natural language expected: ~1.0. Fit quality: moderate.

reasoning: If the zodiac section is genuinely label-oriented, its word-frequency distribution should exhibit a sharper high-frequency peak and fewer hapax legomena than other sections â€” a testable structural prediction that would confirm or deny the label-encoding explanation for its low entropy without requiring decipherment.
